title: 22-23 Math Dept Ann Program Review
text:
ANNUAL PROGRAM REVIEW FOR DEPARTMENT OF MATHEMATICS
2022-2023
by
Wenli Chang & Shirley Louie
PART 1: Describe annual progress / changes / events in the program.
There is only one change this year in the department of Mathematics at College of the
Siskiyous compared to last year. The department has begun to offer Math 1050, Finite
Mathematic, again after having not offered it for several years. The department continues to
offer two Statistics classes face to face while all other courses are online. There is no definite
date when any of the rest of the math courses will be offered face to face. However, it is
questionable whether some of the courses will have enough student enrollment if offered only
face to face.
PART 2: Describe progress on assessing SLOs and PLOs for the program.
Instructors are assessing outcomes, but do not know to whom this information should be
reported or how it should be reported. Even though there was some limited training offered last
year as well as this year on how to enter this information into Elumen, the department has not
been told that we should do so yet.
PART 3: Discuss any support or obstacles encountered by the program.
The math department has been working on achieving “Low and/or No Cost Textbooks.”
It has been very helpful that the OER committee has been offering information as well as
educational sessions about Open Education Resource materials. The distance learning
committee has also been very helpful in providing useful information concerning the teaching of
online courses. There has been a lot of support for instructors teaching online courses.
Part 4: Describe program budgetary needs or implications.
There is no new information for this category either. Mathematics department does not
have a lot of money in its budget. This limits the professional develop opportunities that
instructors can attend. Also, if in the future student enrollment increases, since there are only two
full time instructors, there may be a need at some point to hire another full time instructor.

-- 1 of 1 --
